name: Dumbbell Triceps Kickback
resistance_profile: With the upper arm held parallel to the floor, resistance torque peaks as the elbow reaches full extension — a strong peak-contraction / shortened stimulus.
cues: Keep the upper arm still and parallel to the floor
execution_steps: Hinge at the hips with a flat back, supporting the non-working hand on a bench, and hold a dumbbell in the working hand.
Raise the upper arm until it is parallel to the floor and pinned to your side, elbow bent to about 90 degrees.
Extend the elbow to straighten the arm behind you, squeezing the triceps at the top.
Lower the dumbbell under control back to 90 degrees without dropping the upper arm.
good_when: You want a peak-contraction finisher for the triceps
Building the mind-muscle connection with light, controlled reps
bad_when: You want to load the stretched position of the triceps
You cannot hold a stable braced hinge position
common_errors: Dropping the upper arm and turning it into a row
Using momentum to swing the weight up
progressions: Add load or slow the eccentric
Hold the contracted position for a beat
regressions: Switch to a cable or band kickback for smoother resistance
